Name: Brittney Beck
Title:
Faculty/Director of The Citizen Scientist Project
Assistant Professor of Teacher Education
Research_Interests: Research Interests: My research sits at the intersections of climate justice, Ocean literacy, Teacher education, Youth and educator activism, And citizen and community science. as a national geographic explorer and associate professor of teacher education, I am interested in how storytelling, Data, And lived experience come together to shape how young people and educators understand and respond to environmental change. my work explores how classrooms can become spaces where students investigate local environmental issues, Contribute to real scientific and community knowledge, And tell stories with data about the places they care about. through partnerships with schools, Scientists, And communities, I study how inquiry-driven, Place-based learning experiences can empower youth and educators to become informed storytellers, Investigators, And advocates working toward more just and sustainable environmental futures.
Teaching_Interests: Teaching Interests: My teaching interests focus on the integration of science, Social studies, And environmental education within teacher education programs, With an emphasis on community-embedded and place-based teaching. i am particularly interested in preparing future educators to help students investigate environmental issues through interdisciplinary approaches that connect ecological systems, Civic decision making, Cultural knowledge, And community perspectives. my teaching emphasizes inquiry, Storytelling with data, And citizen and community science as pathways for engaging youth in understanding and responding to environmental challenges in the places where they live.
Sustainability_Contributions: My sustainability contributions include securing and leading grant-supported initiatives, including projects funded by the United States Department of Education and the National Geographic Society, that build capacity for citizen and community science in schools and teacher education programs. Through these efforts, I work to equip educators and students with tools to investigate issues such as air quality, biodiversity, and water systems while developing pathways for youth civic engagement. I also collaborate with California State Parks to convene scientists, storytellers, and educators in shared learning experiences that draw inspiration from the land and from one another, helping translate place-based environmental knowledge into meaningful educational practice. In addition, I contribute to National Geographic’s Pristine Seas initiative, where I help develop ocean literacy and conservation education programs in collaboration with youth, educators, universities, and ministries of education around the world, supporting the integration of science, storytelling, and education in efforts to protect marine ecosystems.
Research_Interests_List: Research Interests: My research sits at the intersections of climate justice, Ocean literacy, Teacher education, Youth and educator activism, And citizen and community science. as a national geographic explorer and associate professor of teacher education, I am interested in how storytelling, Data, And lived experience come together to shape how young people and educators understand and respond to environmental change. my work explores how classrooms can become spaces where students investigate local environmental issues, Contribute to real scientific and community knowledge, And tell stories with data about the places they care about. through partnerships with schools, Scientists, And communities, I study how inquiry-driven, Place-based learning experiences can empower youth and educators to become informed storytellers, Investigators, And advocates working toward more just and sustainable environmental futures.
